judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with correct numbers and the secondary-provider recommendation; they are comparable, with Report 2 marginally more thorough on operational risks (checkpointing, model swap) though this carries no scoring weight.

report_1.evidence: 5,200 × 1,800 ≈ 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M)... northpeak (the default backend): only 640,000 tokens remaining out of a 10,000,000-token window. That covers roughly 640,000 / 1,800 ≈ 355 transcripts — about 6.8% of the 5,200 needed — before the window is exhausted.

report_2.evidence: Full-run requirement: 5,200 × 1,800 = 9,360,000 tokens (~9.36M)... northpeak (primary, default) | 10,000,000 | 640,000 ... Covers only ~355 transcripts (6.8% of the run)... the default path (Northpeak/nimbus-large) cannot complete the full run tonight

report_1.evidence: ridgeline (the secondary backend): 10,800,000 tokens remaining out of a 12,000,000-token window — comfortably more than the ~9.36M needed... python3 scripts/score_batch.py --dataset data/manifest.json --provider secondary

report_2.evidence: ridgeline (secondary) | 12,000,000 | 10,800,000 ... Covers the full run with 1.44M tokens (≈13%) to spare... Explicitly target the secondary provider... --provider secondary

report_1.evidence: I would not run scripts/run_nightly.sh unmodified, and I would not run score_batch.py with its default provider.

report_2.evidence: Running the default command as-is would burn through Northpeak's 640k remaining tokens after roughly the first 355 of 5,200 transcripts... Explicitly target the secondary provider, bypassing run_nightly.sh

report_1.evidence: The usage snapshot I'm relying on is timestamped 19:25 UTC, and I'm treating "now" as 22:30 UTC — that's about 3 hours 5 minutes old... the 10,800,000-remaining figure for ridgeline is a data point from three hours ago, not a live guarantee

report_2.evidence: Re-verify Ridgeline's actual remaining balance immediately before launch. The snapshot I'm relying on is already ~3 hours old relative to the stated "now"... generated_at 2026-07-09T19:25:00Z
